title: C++ Data Types
content:
### Data Types in C++

C++ offers a rich set of built-in data types and allows user-defined types.

---

### Basic Data Types

| Type | Size | Range | Description |
|------|------|-------|-------------|
| `bool` | 1 byte | true/false | Boolean value |
| `char` | 1 byte | -128 to 127 or 0 to 255 | Single character |
| `int` | 4 bytes | -2,147,483,648 to 2,147,483,647 | Integer |
| `float` | 4 bytes | ~7 decimal digits | Floating point |
| `double` | 8 bytes | ~15 decimal digits | Double precision |
| `void` | 0 bytes | No value | Absence of type |

---

### Modifiers

```cpp
short int a;        // 2 bytes
long int b;         // 4 or 8 bytes
long long int c;    // 8 bytes
unsigned int d;     // Non-negative only
signed int e;       // Explicitly signed
```

---

### Type Sizes (C++11 <cstdint>)

```cpp
#include <cstdint>

int8_t   i8;   // Exactly 8 bits
int16_t  i16;  // Exactly 16 bits
int32_t  i32;  // Exactly 32 bits
int64_t  i64;  // Exactly 64 bits
uint32_t ui32; // Unsigned 32 bits
```

---

### Checking Type Size

```cpp
cout << sizeof(int) << endl;       // 4
cout << sizeof(long long) << endl; // 8
```

---

> **Key Point:** Type sizes can vary by platform. Use `<cstdint>` for fixed-width types when portability is important.

quiz.question: What is the typical size of an int in C++?
